text.title: Vector Database Selection
text.summary: Selection decision tree by primary constraint, per-database strengths and weaknesses, and use-case routing rules.

rule.statement: Choose the database by primary constraint first; do not default to the most popular option without matching constraints.
rule.rationale: Each database has a structural trade-off. Pinecone has zero ops burden but vendor lock-in. Chroma is trivial to start but fails in production. pgvector fits existing PostgreSQL but degrades above 10M vectors.

list.item: Need managed service, zero ops → Pinecone (serverless, predictable pricing)
Have existing PostgreSQL infrastructure → pgvector (SQL integration, familiar tooling, ACID)
Rapid prototyping / local development → Chroma (simple API, in-memory)
Production self-hosted, complex filtering + moderate scale → Qdrant (Rust perf, excellent filters)
Production self-hosted, knowledge graph features → Weaviate (GraphQL, cross-references)
Extreme scale 1B+ vectors → Milvus (distributed, GPU support, CNCF)
Enterprise compliance (HIPAA, SOC2) → Milvus/Zilliz or Weaviate Cloud

section.title: Database Profiles
section.p: Qdrant: Rust-based, excellent payload filtering, native hybrid search (dense + sparse), binary quantization (32x memory reduction), strong multi-tenancy. Best for production RAG with complex metadata filtering.
Weaviate: GraphQL API, native knowledge graph with cross-references, built-in vectorizers (text2vec modules), BM25 + vector hybrid. Best for document relationship modeling.
Milvus: highest throughput (100K+ QPS), most indexing strategies (IVF, HNSW, DiskANN, GPU), distributed architecture, tiered storage. Best for enterprise scale and billion-vector datasets.
Pinecone: true serverless, zero ops, consistent low latency, simple SDK. Best for teams without DevOps resources. Weakness: vendor lock-in, no self-hosting, limited filtering.
pgvector: existing PostgreSQL infrastructure, full SQL (JOINs, transactions), ACID, Django/Rails ORM support. Degrades significantly above ~100M vectors without sharding.
Chroma: simplest API, in-process, no network overhead. Not suitable for production multi-user workloads or datasets above ~1M vectors.

list.item: RAG chatbot → Qdrant or Weaviate (hybrid search, metadata filtering)
E-commerce similarity → Milvus or Pinecone (scale, real-time updates)
Existing Django/Rails app → pgvector (no new infrastructure)
Multi-tenant SaaS → Qdrant (namespace isolation per collection or payload filtering)
Image/video similarity → Milvus (GPU acceleration, large vectors)
